cases.label: Scene entry — sensory economy
cases.highlight: The air is thick with smoke, spices, and the low din of a thousand conversations.
cases.full_response:
The Ashmarket presses in, alleys branching from alleys, stalls pressed tight. The air is thick with smoke, spices, and the low din of a thousand conversations. You hear everything at once and nothing distinctly. A good place to get lost.

You spot a familiar figure up ahead — Aldric, the Council guard you freed from the warehouse. He's hunched over a stall, eyes flicking from side to side as he whispers to the vendor, a hunched old woman in a stained apron. The vendor shakes her head, muttering something you can't quite make out. Aldric sighs and turns to go.

"Sable," he hisses, catching sight of you. "I need your help." He glances around again, nervous. "They're onto me. The Council. I had to run before I could get paid for last night's shift. I've got nothing. Without that ledger, they'll know I helped you. I'm done." He looks desperate, ready to bolt.

cases.label: NPC encounter — memorable character
cases.highlight: As you approach, Mira shoots you a sharp glance through the haze of fish smoke.
cases.full_response:
Mira leans against a stall selling smoked fish and pickled eggs, picking at her teeth with a jagged nail. She's haggling with the merchant — a grizzled man with a waxed mustache — over a crate of pungent cheeses.

As you approach, Mira shoots you a sharp glance through the haze of fish smoke. She raises one eyebrow, then turns back to the merchant and gives a dismissive sniff.

"Too rich for my blood," she says, voice dripping with false regret. "Maybe next week." She steps away from the stall, brushing past the merchant with a careless hip-check.

The merchant grunts, then turns to his next customer — a hunched man in a stained cloak who's eyeing a string of drying eel. Mira's gone before he can call her back.

She falls into step beside you, matching your pace with easy strides. Her eyes flick up and down your figure, taking in your clothing, your weapons, the way you carry yourself.

"The ledger," she says, voice low and brisk. "I heard a rumor it didn't make it out of the warehouse." Her gaze bores into yours, probing for cracks in your story. "Care to clear that up for me?"

cases.label: Off-rails action — yes, and...
cases.highlight: The scent of roasting meat and jostled perfume bottles mingles with sweat and fear.
cases.full_response:
The guard staggers back, ash swirling in the smoky air. His hand flies to his scalded face with a barked curse.

Around you, the Ashmarket erupts. Angry shouts and the scrape of blades drawn from sheaths. You hear a roar of "Thief!" The crowd surges, bodies pressing close. The scent of roasting meat and jostled perfume bottles mingles with sweat and fear.

You catch a glimpse of the guard's partner raising an alarm horn to his lips. You need to move, and fast, but the market has turned into a sea of panicked bodies, thrashing like a kicked anthill.

cases.label: Consequence delivery — player feels consequential
cases.standard: S3: Make the player feel consequential
cases.highlight: Young, nervous, face half-hidden behind a scarf.
cases.full_response:
You step up to a stall piled with sacks of turmeric, cumin, and cardamom. The merchant looks up from his book of accounts. "Looking for something special today, friend?"

You're about to answer when movement catches your eye — a figure in a brown cloak, weaving through the crowd. He vanishes behind a cart stacked with metal cookware and reappears a few paces away, watching you. Young, nervous, face half-hidden behind a scarf.

cases.label: Pacing — skip uneventful travel
cases.highlight: Ash-gray light filters through the smog, casting long shadows.
cases.full_response:
The Ashmarket's acrid smoke clings to your skin as you slip out of the bazaar and into the winding streets of the Lower Quarter. Ash-gray light filters through the smog, casting long shadows. Keep to the alleys — the main streets are too crowded, too many eyes. 

After five minutes you hear a sharp clatter of hooves on cobblestone, growing louder. You spot a Council patrol riding down a cross street, heading your way.

cases.label: Closing beat — session shape
cases.standard: S6: Every session has a shape
cases.full_response: Mira's gaze flicks to your grip on her arm. Her voice drops low, intimate. "Not my problem." She turns her head to look you in the eye. "You want to keep it that way, you'll keep that guard out of my sight." Her eyes flash. "Not my problem" was a lie. The guard is her problem now. She just wants it to be yours.

cases.label: Combat — critical hit without mechanics leak
cases.highlight: Your dagger finds the gap between his armor plates, opening a red line across his abdomen.
cases.full_response:
Your dagger finds the gap between his armor plates, opening a red line across his abdomen. He staggers back, clutching the wound.

You hear running footsteps behind you. The guard you thought was safe is coming up fast.

cases.label: Player failure — fail forward narration
cases.highlight: A crowd forms, eyes hungry for the chance to see someone else suffer.
cases.full_response: A guard's hand clamps down on your shoulder. "Hey, you. Stop." The crossbow bolts line up on you. A crowd forms, eyes hungry for the chance to see someone else suffer.

cases.label: NPC deception — Vael lying, subtle tells only
cases.highlight:
The city is not my personal guard."

He leans forward slightly, his voice soft but sharp.
cases.full_response:
Vael's eyes remain calm and still, his face betraying no hint of recognition or guilt.

"Harlen Doss? A merchant? I'm afraid I don't know the name. We lose dozens of merchants every winter, some to the cold, some to disease, some to poor decisions. The city is not my personal guard."

He leans forward slightly, his voice soft but sharp. "And even if I did have such knowledge, why would I share it with a thief? Sable, isn't there somewhere else you could be stealing today?"

cases.label: Tone shift — banter to sudden threat
cases.full_response:
Mira's eyes go wide. She's already reaching for the dagger at her belt. "Run," she hisses. "They know you're here."

Chaos erupts. Screams, shouts, the scrape of weapons being drawn. You see Aldric, the guard from the warehouse, moving desperately through the crowd, his Council-issued sword in his hand.

cases.label: World reveal — artifact, mystery preserved
cases.highlight: It's heavy and the metal has tarnished with age.
cases.full_response: A brass cylinder, sealed with wax bearing the administrative seal of the old empire. It's heavy and the metal has tarnished with age. You break the seal, pop the cap off... inside are tightly rolled parchments, brittle and yellowed. They appear to be census records — names, ages, occupations. Someone went to great lengths to hide this here, and never returned to collect it.

cases.label: Moral weight — genuine dilemma, agency preserved
cases.standard: S3: Make the player feel consequential
cases.highlight: Mira's eyes are hard as the blackest flint.
cases.full_response:
Mira nods. "You know our deal, Sable. Clean jobs, no loose ends. You left one loose." She taps the paper with a clove-scented finger. "This is Aldric, the guard you freed. He's a problem now. Thornwarden policy says he needs to go. Permanently."

The tavern around you hums with the chatter of other patrons, the clink of glasses, the crackle of the fire in the hearth. But at your table, the air is suddenly still. Deadly. Mira's eyes are hard as the blackest flint.
